content.json:
# Lateral Movement Hunting
**MITRE ATT&CK:** T1021.001 RDP · T1021.002 SMB/Admin Shares · T1550 Use Alternate Auth Material · T1078 Valid Accounts

**Data source:** `SecurityEvent` (Windows Security log via AMA). Logon types: 3 = network, 10 = RemoteInteractive (RDP).

> A single account authenticating to many hosts in a short window is the classic lateral-movement signal.

content.query: SecurityEvent | where EventID == 4624 and LogonType == 10 | summarize Count=count(), Sources=make_set(IpAddress,20) by Account, Computer | sort by Count desc
content.title: Inbound RDP logons (LogonType 10) — T1021.001

content.query: SecurityEvent | where EventID == 4624 and LogonType in (3,10) | summarize Hosts=dcount(Computer), HostList=make_set(Computer,30), First=min(TimeGenerated), Last=max(TimeGenerated) by Account | where Hosts >= 5 and Account !endswith '$' | sort by Hosts desc
content.title: Accounts authenticating to many hosts — T1021/T1078

content.query: SecurityEvent | where EventID == 4625 | summarize Failures=count(), Accounts=dcount(Account) by IpAddress, Computer | where Failures >= 10 | sort by Failures desc
content.title: Failed logon bursts (possible spread/brute force) — T1110
